preamble: The diphthong /ei/, typically pronounced as a glide from a mid-front unrounded vowel to a close-front unrounded vowel, can undergo a transformation into the close front rounded vowel /y/. This shift involves two primary phonetic changes: monophthongization, where the diphthong simplifies into a single vowel, followed by rounding of that vowel.
phoneticEffects: Monophthongization, Rounding, Fronting (if the monophthongized vowel was not already fully front)

examples.note: Orthographic representation of the shift in surname development, e.g., from forms like 'Beiler' to 'Byler'.

sources: Byler Surname: Meaning & Origin Of The Last Name.
